cards.prompt: One and the same lambda: assigned to Func it is an executable delegate (del(4)=True), assigned to Expression<Func<>> it is a tree (root NodeType=Lambda, Body=LessThan), so the output is del=True exp.NodeType=Lambda exp.Body=LessThan
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Func<int,bool> del = num => num < 5; Expression<Func<int,bool>> exp = num => num < 5; Console.WriteLine($"del={del(4)} exp.NodeType={exp.NodeType} exp.Body={exp.Body.NodeType}");

cards.prompt: An expression tree can be executed by compiling it into a delegate: exp.Compile() yields Func<int,bool>, f(4) is 4<5 True and f(9) is False, so the output is True False
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int,bool>> exp = num => num < 5; Func<int,bool> f = exp.Compile(); Console.WriteLine($"{f(4)} {f(9)}");

cards.prompt: The tree is decomposed by properties: parameter num, body is a BinaryExpression with NodeType=LessThan, left leaf is parameter num, right leaf is constant 5, so the output is num => num LessThan 5
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq.Expressions; Expression<Func<int,bool>> exprTree = num => num < 5; var param=(ParameterExpression)exprTree.Parameters[0]; var op=(BinaryExpression)exprTree.Body; var left=(ParameterExpression)op.Left; var right=(ConstantExpression)op.Right; Console.WriteLine($"{param.Name} => {left.Name} {op.NodeType} {right.Value}");
